applied_optimizations.name: Fused subtract-tanh-subtract Triton kernel
applied_optimizations.description: Fused three elementwise operations (subtract1, tanh, subtract2) into a single Triton kernel. Uses manual tanh implementation with tl.math.exp since tl.libdevice.tanh is unavailable on ROCm. BLOCK_SIZE=1024 aligned to AMD wavefront size (64).

attempted_but_failed.name: Custom Triton avgpool2d kernel
attempted_but_failed.reason: Triton kernel with nested for-loops was 10x slower than PyTorch's optimized avg_pool2d (483ms vs 4.1ms). The overhead of launching one thread per output element and iterating over the pooling window in Triton was too high.

notes: tl.libdevice.* functions are UNAVAILABLE on ROCm Triton - must use manual implementations
Manual tanh: x_clamped = max(min(x, 10), -10); exp_2x = tl.math.exp(2*x); tanh = (exp_2x-1)/(exp_2x+1)
BLOCK_SIZE should be multiple of 64 for AMD MI355X wavefront alignment
PyTorch's nn.AvgPool2d is highly optimized - custom Triton kernel was 100x slower
Fusing elementwise ops gives ~1.19x speedup for this problem
